title: To A Lady - With Flowers From A Roman Wall
content:
Take these flowers which, purple waving,
    On the ruin'd rampart grew,
    Where, the sons of freedom braving,
    Rome's imperial standards flew.

    Warriors from the breach of danger
    Pluck no longer laurels there;
    They but yield the passing stranger
    Wild-flower wreaths the Beauty's hair.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Walter Scott (Sir) delivers a powerful performance in "To A Lady - With Flowers From A Roman Wall"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
